titleStmt.title: Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 42

div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, Hugh de Mortimer sends Greeting: Let those to come as well as those present know that I have granted and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the Canons of the Church of St Andrews, the Chapel of Fowlis together with the land that went with it before Fowlis was given to William Maule my grandfather, and with ten acres of land which the same William gave to the said Chapel as an addition, and with teinds and oblations, and with everything pertaining to the same Chapel, in perpetual alms, for the welfare of my soul, and of my wife’s, and for the souls of my ancestors and successors. Wherefore I firmly will that the aforesaid Canons hold and possess the aforementioned Chapel free and quit from every secular service and exaction, and without my or my heirs’ claiming it back, just as freely and quietly as they hold and possess their other alms in Scot[ia or Scotland]. I have also allowed to the said Canons the teind of my mill and common pasture according to the easement of my feu at Fowlis, namely, for eight oxen, and ten cows, and three horses, and 100 sheep. I have also allowed to the same Canons that there be support of the said livestock in that pasture for one year, the first after giving birth, just as it is more fully set out in the charter of the aforesaid William; and that this my grant should maintain the force of perpetual endurance, I have caused it to be confirmed by the reinforcement of the present written document with the application of my seal. With theses witnesses: the lord William, bishop of St Andrews; Master Laurence, archdeacon of St Andrews; Master Adam Ovid; Master Alexander de Saint-Martin; Master David of Bernham; the lord Simon de Noisy; William of Gullen; John of Haddington; and many others.
